Question: Use the definition to calculate the derivative of the function $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = ( 2 t^{2} + 3 ) \mathbf{\text{i}} + ( 5 t - 6 ) \mathbf{\text{j}} .$$
Calculate the derivative of the function $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = ( t \text{ln} t ) \mathbf{\text{i}} + ( 5 e^{t} ) \mathbf{\text{j}} + ( \text{cos} t - \text{sin} t ) \mathbf{\text{k}} .$$

Given the vector-valued functions $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = \text{cos} t \mathbf{\text{i}} + \text{sin} t \mathbf{\text{j}} - e^{2 t} \mathbf{\text{k}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{u}} ( t ) = t \mathbf{\text{i}} + \text{sin} t \mathbf{\text{j}} + \text{cos} t \mathbf{\text{k}} ,$$ calculate $$\frac{d}{d t} [ \mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) \cdotp \mathbf{r^{\prime}} ( t ) ]$$ and $$\frac{d}{d t} [ \mathbf{\text{u}} ( t ) \times \mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) ] .$$
Answer: $$\frac{d}{d t} [ \mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) \cdotp \mathbf{r^{\prime}} ( t ) ] = 8 e^{4 t}$$ $$\frac{d}{d t} \left[\right. \text{u} \left(\right. t \left.\right) \times \text{r} \left(\right. t \left.\right) \left]\right. \\ = - \left(\right. e^{2 t} \left(\right. \text{cos} t + 2 \text{sin} t \left.\right) + \text{cos} 2 t \left.\right) \text{i} + \left(\right. e^{2 t} \left(\right. 2 t + 1 \left.\right) - \text{sin} 2 t \left.\right) \text{j} + \left(\right. t \text{cos} t + \text{sin} t - \text{cos} 2 t \left.\right) \text{k}$$

Question: Find the unit tangent vector for the vector-valued function $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = ( t^{2} - 3 ) \mathbf{\text{i}} + ( 2 t + 1 ) \mathbf{\text{j}} + ( t - 2 ) \mathbf{\text{k}} .$$
Calculate the following integral: $$\int_{1}^{3} [ ( 2 t + 4 ) \mathbf{\text{i}} + ( 3 t^{2} - 4 t ) \mathbf{\text{j}} ] d t .$$
